atomic.h(3) Library Functions Manual atomic.h(3)

NAME

atomic.h - ⚛️ Atomic operations abstraction layer with debug tracking

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct atomic_t
Atomic value wrapper for integral/boolean types. struct atomic_ptr_t
Atomic pointer wrapper.

Macros


#define ATOMIC_REGISTER_AUTO(name, parent_ptr)
Register an atomic variable for debug tracking with automatic name generation. #define ATOMIC_PTR_REGISTER_AUTO(name)
Register an atomic pointer variable for debug tracking with automatic name generation. #define ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO(...)
Initialize an atomic_t with automatic named registration. #define ATOMIC_PTR_INIT_AUTO(...)
Initialize an atomic_ptr_t with automatic named registration. #define _atomic_init_auto(name)
#define _atomic_ptr_init_auto(name)

Functions


bool atomic_load_bool (atomic_t *a)
Atomically load a boolean value. bool atomic_load_bool_impl (const atomic_t *a)
void atomic_store_bool (atomic_t *a, bool value)
Atomically store a boolean value. void atomic_store_bool_impl (atomic_t *a, uint64_t value)
bool atomic_cas_bool (atomic_t *a, bool *expected, bool new_value)
Atomically compare-and-swap a boolean. bool atomic_cas_bool_impl (atomic_t *a, uint64_t *expected, uint64_t new_value)
bool atomic_exchange_bool (atomic_t *a, bool new_value)
Atomically exchange a boolean and return the old value. bool atomic_exchange_bool_impl (atomic_t *a, bool new_value)
int atomic_load_int (atomic_t *a)
Atomically load an int value. int atomic_load_int_impl (const atomic_t *a)
void atomic_store_int (atomic_t *a, int value)
Atomically store an int value. void atomic_store_int_impl (atomic_t *a, uint64_t value)
int atomic_fetch_add_int (atomic_t *a, int delta)
Atomically add to an int and return the previous value. int atomic_fetch_add_int_impl (atomic_t *a, int64_t delta)
int atomic_fetch_sub_int (atomic_t *a, int delta)
Atomically subtract from an int and return the previous value. int atomic_fetch_sub_int_impl (atomic_t *a, int64_t delta)
bool atomic_cas_int (atomic_t *a, int *expected, int new_value)
Atomically compare-and-swap an int. bool atomic_cas_int_impl (atomic_t *a, int64_t *expected, int64_t new_value)
uint64_t atomic_load_u64 (atomic_t *a)
Atomically load a uint64_t value. uint64_t atomic_load_u64_impl (const atomic_t *a)
void atomic_store_u64 (atomic_t *a, uint64_t value)
Atomically store a uint64_t value. void atomic_store_u64_impl (atomic_t *a, uint64_t value)
uint64_t atomic_fetch_add_u64 (atomic_t *a, uint64_t delta)
Atomically add to a uint64_t and return the previous value. uint64_t atomic_fetch_add_u64_impl (atomic_t *a, uint64_t delta)
uint64_t atomic_fetch_sub_u64 (atomic_t *a, uint64_t delta)
Atomically subtract from a uint64_t and return the previous value. uint64_t atomic_fetch_sub_u64_impl (atomic_t *a, uint64_t delta)
bool atomic_cas_u64 (atomic_t *a, uint64_t *expected, uint64_t new_value)
Atomically compare-and-swap a uint64_t. bool atomic_cas_u64_impl (atomic_t *a, uint64_t *expected, uint64_t new_value)
uint64_t atomic_exchange_u64 (atomic_t *a, uint64_t new_value)
Atomically exchange a uint64_t and return the old value. uint64_t atomic_exchange_u64_impl (atomic_t *a, uint64_t new_value)
void * atomic_ptr_load (atomic_ptr_t *a)
Atomically load a pointer. void * atomic_ptr_load_impl (const atomic_ptr_t *a)
void atomic_ptr_store (atomic_ptr_t *a, void *value)
Atomically store a pointer. void atomic_ptr_store_impl (atomic_ptr_t *a, void *value)
bool atomic_ptr_cas (atomic_ptr_t *a, void **expected, void *new_value)
Atomically compare-and-swap a pointer. bool atomic_ptr_cas_impl (atomic_ptr_t *a, void **expected, void *new_value)
void * atomic_ptr_exchange (atomic_ptr_t *a, void *new_value)
Atomically exchange a pointer and return the old value. void * atomic_ptr_exchange_impl (atomic_ptr_t *a, void *new_value)
int atomic_exchange_int (atomic_t *a, int new_value)
Atomically exchange an int and return the old value. int atomic_exchange_int_impl (atomic_t *a, int new_value)
void atomic_on_load (atomic_t *a)
void atomic_on_store (atomic_t *a)
void atomic_on_cas (atomic_t *a, bool success)
void atomic_on_fetch (atomic_t *a)
void atomic_ptr_on_load (atomic_ptr_t *a)
void atomic_ptr_on_store (atomic_ptr_t *a)
void atomic_ptr_on_cas (atomic_ptr_t *a, bool success)
void atomic_ptr_on_exchange (atomic_ptr_t *a)
void debug_atomic_init (void)
void debug_atomic_shutdown (void)
bool debug_atomic_is_initialized (void)
void debug_atomic_print_state (void)

Detailed Description

⚛️ Atomic operations abstraction layer with debug tracking

Date

March 2026

Provides a platform-independent wrapper for atomic operations similar to mutex_t, rwlock_t, and cond_t abstractions. Supports named registration, debug tracking, and integration with --sync-state output.

Design principles:

  • No recursion (atomics never depend on other atomics)
  • No mutexes in implementation (uses raw C11 atomics internally)
  • Named registration via named.c (NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC macro)
  • Debug-only timing and count tracking
  • Exchange operations for atomic min/max patterns (avoids TOCTOU races)

Supported types via typed macros:

  • bool: atomic_load_bool, atomic_store_bool, atomic_cas_bool, atomic_exchange_bool
  • int: atomic_load_int, atomic_store_int, atomic_fetch_add_int, atomic_fetch_sub_int
  • uint64_t: atomic_load_u64, atomic_store_u64, atomic_fetch_add_u64, atomic_fetch_sub_u64, atomic_exchange_u64
  • Pointers: atomic_ptr_load, atomic_ptr_store, atomic_ptr_cas, atomic_ptr_exchange

Usage:

// Declare a named atomic
atomic_t g_should_exit = ATOMIC_INIT(false, "g_should_exit");
// Load/store operations
bool value = atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit);
atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
// In release builds: zero overhead, calls _impl functions directly
// In debug builds: includes registration and timing hooks

Definition in file atomic.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define _atomic_init_auto( name)

Value:

{0}; \
static void __attribute__((constructor, used)) _atomic_reg_##name(void) { \


  NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&name, #name, NULL); \
} typedef int _atomic_assert_##name




Definition at line 528 of file atomic.h.


528 #define _atomic_init_auto(name) {0}; \
529   static void __attribute__((constructor, used)) _atomic_reg_##name(void) { \
530     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&name, #name, NULL); \
531   } typedef int _atomic_assert_##name










#define
  _atomic_ptr_init_auto( name)


Value:


{0}; \
static void __attribute__((constructor, used)) _atomic_ptr_reg_##name(void) { \


  NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC_PTR(&name, #name, NULL); \
} typedef int _atomic_ptr_assert_##name






Definition at line 533 of file atomic.h.


533 #define _atomic_ptr_init_auto(name) {0}; \
534   static void __attribute__((constructor, used)) _atomic_ptr_reg_##name(void) { \
535     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC_PTR(&name, #name, NULL); \
536   } typedef int _atomic_ptr_assert_##name










#define
  ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO( ...)


Value:


= _atomic_init_auto(__VA_ARGS__)






Initialize an atomic_t with automatic named registration.
    In debug builds, automatically registers the atomic with named.c using a
    constructor function. In release builds, just initializes to {0}.




Usage:




// Declare with automatic registration:
static atomic_t g_counter ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO("g_counter");
// Or simpler - use the variable name directly:
static atomic_t g_counter ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO();



Note: Currently requires GCC/Clang constructor attribute. For
    other compilers, use ATOMIC_REGISTER_AUTO(variable_name)
  separately.


Definition at line 510 of file atomic.h.






#define
  ATOMIC_PTR_INIT_AUTO( ...)


Value:


= _atomic_ptr_init_auto(__VA_ARGS__)






Initialize an atomic_ptr_t with automatic named
    registration. Same as ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO but for atomic_ptr_t.


Definition at line 521 of file atomic.h.






#define
  ATOMIC_PTR_REGISTER_AUTO( name)


Value:


NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC_PTR(&(name), #name)






Register an atomic pointer variable for debug tracking with
    automatic name generation. Same as ATOMIC_REGISTER_AUTO but for _Atomic(void
    *) variables.




Usage:




_Atomic(void *) g_buffer = {0};
ATOMIC_PTR_REGISTER_AUTO(g_buffer);



Definition at line 485 of file atomic.h.






#define
  ATOMIC_REGISTER_AUTO( name, parent_ptr)


Value:


NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&(name), #name, (uintptr_t)(const void *)(parent_ptr))






Register an atomic variable for debug tracking with automatic name
    generation. Uses stringification to avoid repeating the variable name.
    Simplifies the declaration + registration pattern.




Usage:




// In header:
extern atomic_t g_counter;
// In source:
atomic_t g_counter = {0};
// In main/init:
ATOMIC_REGISTER_AUTO(g_counter, NULL);



Definition at line 472 of file atomic.h.


Referenced by server_main().








Function
  Documentation




bool atomic_cas_bool (atomic_t * a, bool *
  expected, bool new_value)


Atomically compare-and-swap a boolean.


Parameters


a Pointer to atomic_t


expected Expected current value (passed by reference)


new_value Value to store if comparison succeeds


Returns


true if swap succeeded, false otherwise


Definition at line 159 of file atomic.c.


159                                                                   {
160     if (!a || !expected) return false;
161     uint64_t exp = (uint64_t)*expected;
162     bool success = atomic_compare_exchange_strong((_Atomic(uint64_t) *)&a->impl, &exp, (uint64_t)new_value);
163     *expected = (bool)exp;
164     atomic_on_cas(a, success);
165     return success;
166 }






References atomic_on_cas(), and bool.


Referenced by handle_image_frame_packet(),
    keyboard_help_check_signal_cancel(), log_lock_terminal(),
    log_search_check_signal_cancel(), splash_intro_start(), and
    symbol_cache_resolve_batch().

38 }

bool atomic_exchange_bool (atomic_t * a,
  bool new_value)


Atomically exchange a boolean and return the old value.


Parameters


a Pointer to atomic_t


new_value Boolean value to store


Returns


Previous boolean value


Atomically swaps the new value for the old value and returns the
    old value. Avoids TOCTOU race conditions present in load+check+cas
  patterns.


Definition at line 265 of file atomic.c.


265                                                        {
266     if (!a) return false;
267     bool result = (bool)atomic_exchange((_Atomic(uint64_t) *)&a->impl, (uint64_t)new_value);
268     atomic_on_store(a);
269     return result;
270 }






References atomic_on_store(), and bool.


Referenced by disconnect_client_for_bad_data(),
    display_disable_logging_for_first_frame(),
    handle_image_frame_h265_packet(), and
    session_display_write_ascii().

  bool new_value)

126     if (!a) return false;
127     return (bool)atomic_exchange((_Atomic(uint64_t) *)&a->impl, (uint64_t)new_value);
128 }

uint64_t atomic_exchange_u64 (atomic_t * a,
  uint64_t new_value)


Atomically exchange a uint64_t and return the old
  value.


Parameters


a Pointer to atomic_t


new_value Value to store


Returns


Previous uint64_t value


Used for atomic min/max updates and other exchange patterns where
    you need the old value atomically. Avoids TOCTOU race conditions present in
    load+check+cas.




Example (atomic minimum):




uint64_t old = atomic_exchange_u64(&stats->min_val, 0);
// old is the previous value, atomically



Definition at line 279 of file atomic.c.


279                                                               {
280     if (!a) return 0;
281     uint64_t result = atomic_exchange((_Atomic(uint64_t) *)&a->impl, new_value);
282     atomic_on_store(a);
283     return result;
284 }






References atomic_on_store().






uint64_t atomic_exchange_u64_impl (atomic_t * a,
  uint64_t new_value)


Definition at line 135 of file atomic.c.


135                                                                    {
136     if (!a) return 0;
137     return atomic_exchange((_Atomic(uint64_t) *)&a->impl, new_value);
138 }

bool atomic_ptr_cas (atomic_ptr_t * a, void **
  expected, void * new_value)


Atomically compare-and-swap a pointer.


Parameters


a Pointer to atomic_ptr_t


expected Expected current pointer (passed by reference)


new_value Pointer value to store if comparison succeeds


Returns


true if swap succeeded, false otherwise

252     if (!a || !expected) return false;
253     bool success = atomic_compare_exchange_strong((_Atomic(void *) *)&a->impl, expected, new_value);

255     return success;
256 }
